2.20: A type: 'The man is a close relative of ours, one of our guardian-redeemers.' Boaz is the OT's most fully developed type of Christ as redeemer: (1) he is near of kin — Christ became our kinsman through the incarnation (Heb 2:14-15: he shared in flesh and blood that through death he might destroy him who has the power of death); (2) he has the right to redeem — as the sinless son of God, Christ alone qualifies; (3) he is willing to redeem — another go'el existed but declined (Ruth 4:6); Christ took on the obligation no one else could fulfill; (4) he pays the redemption price — Boaz redeems the land and takes Ruth; Christ redeems his people by his blood and takes them as his bride (Eph 5:25-27; Rev 19:7-9). The kinsman-redeemer motif is the book of Ruth's entire Christological contribution.
